summary: Executed backend API regression (5 pytest cases) and frontend E2E flows for landing, auth, dashboard, chat, SOS, language toggle, and clinics geolocation.

integration_issues.issue: Clinics UI can remain in loading state for several seconds before map/cards appear (observed one run with no results at 7s wait, then resolved by ~10s+ in isolated run).
Main screen copy is partially untranslated in Hindi mode (example: chat title remains mixed language: 'एआई symptom checker').

issues: Emergency banner helper text remains English ('Use nearby clinic results for quick access and directions.') while surrounding content is Hindi.

action_items: Improve clinics loading UX (timeout/retry state, skeleton-to-result transition) so users don’t see prolonged unresolved loading.
Complete Hindi localization on all primary pages (chat and clinics text fragments still mixed English/Hindi).
Main agent should retest clinics geolocation flow in browser after any UX/loading adjustment.
critical_code_review_comments: Localization is inconsistent across key screens; several user-facing strings are hardcoded English instead of using translations map.
Clinics page relies on async geolocation + remote lookup without intermediate fallback state escalation, causing apparent hanging behavior under slower responses.
seed_data_creation: No additional seed data created; used existing demo account.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend APIs passed via pytest against public URL. Frontend core flows pass; clinics can be slow to resolve in UI and Hindi translation remains partial on some labels/messages.
rca of the issue: RCA 1 (Clinics perceived hang): UI sets loading true and waits on geolocation + remote clinic request; on slower responses the page shows only loading state for several seconds, creating a perceived failure although response eventually succeeds. Repro: login -> /clinics -> observe loading state beyond initial 7s in one run. Mitigation: add staged timeout message/retry prompt and show partial fallback sooner. RCA 2 (Localization gaps): Some strings are hardcoded in components (not fully from translations object), causing mixed-language Hindi views.
